name: Muleteers' Trail
name_original: Fuente de Almazorre por el Camino de Serón de Nágima a Deza – ida y vuelta desde Deza
description: Hike the moderate 5.7-mile Fuente de Almazorre loop from Deza, exploring the Camino de Serón de Nágima and natural monuments.
region_intro: Hiking around Deza offers a variety of trails through a landscape characterized by gentle hills and open countryside. The region features paths that traverse agricultural lands and historical routes, providing views of the local topography. Elevations are generally moderate, with some routes including gradual ascents. The area's trails are suitable for exploring the natural and cultural heritage of the Deza region. Best hiking trails around Deza The most popular hiking route is Alto Navarro – circular desde Cihuela, a 4.0 miles (6.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 51 minutes to complete. This easy circular route offers views of the surrounding high ground. Another top favourite among local hikers is Muleteers' Trail, a moderate 5.7 miles (9.2 km) path. This trail follows historical paths, winding through varied terrain with moderate elevation changes. Local hikers also love the Torrijo de la Cañada loop from Torrijo de la Cañada, a 7.2 miles (11.6 km) trail leading through local villages and countryside, often completed in about 3 hours 24 minutes. Hiking around Deza is defined by open countryside, historical paths, and gentle hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate hikes. The routes in Deza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 7 hikers have used komoot to explore Deza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Deza?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;6&lt;/strong&gt; distinct hiking trails documented around Deza, offering a variety of experiences through the region's landscape.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners in Deza?
A: Yes, Deza offers &lt;strong&gt;2 easy routes&lt;/strong&gt;. A great option for beginners is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1059007413/alto-navarro-circular-desde-cihuela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Navarro – circular desde Cihuela&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.0 miles (6.5 km) long and provides gentle terrain with views of the surrounding high ground.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Deza?
A: The hiking trails around Deza are characterized by gentle hills and open countryside. You'll find paths traversing agricultural lands and historical routes, with generally moderate elevations and gradual ascents.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Deza?
A: Yes, several routes are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31479767" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrijo de la Cañada loop from Torrijo de la Cañada&lt;/a&gt; is a 7.2 miles (11.6 km) circular trail that takes you through local villages and countryside.

Q: What are some longer, more moderate hikes around Deza?
A: For those looking for a moderate challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24550154" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Peñalcazar – Peñalcázar Castle loop from La Alameda&lt;/a&gt; is a good choice. It covers 8.8 miles (14.1 km) with moderate elevation changes, offering a longer exploration of the area.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Deza suitable for families with children?
A: Many of the trails in Deza are suitable for families, especially the easier routes with moderate terrain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1059007413/alto-navarro-circular-desde-cihuela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Navarro – circular desde Cihuela&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent family-friendly option due to its manageable length and gentle slopes.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Deza?
A: While specific regulations vary, generally, dogs are welcome on many of the open countryside trails in Deza, especially if kept on a leash. Always be mindful of agricultural areas and local wildlife.

Q: What can I see along the hiking trails in Deza?
A: Hikers can explore historical sites and natural features. Notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5335428" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle and Church of San Millán de Berdejo&lt;/a&gt; and the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2683451" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monteagudo de las Vicarías Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. You'll also pass through charming settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4394230" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrijo de la Cañada&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Deza?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of &lt;strong&gt;5.0 stars&lt;/strong&gt;. Hikers often praise the peaceful open countryside, the historical paths, and the variety of routes suitable for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any trails that follow historical paths in Deza?
A: Yes, the region is known for its historical routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058997977/muleteers-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muleteers' Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, following ancient paths through varied terrain, offering a glimpse into the area's past.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Deza?
A: Deza's landscape, characterized by open countryside, is generally pleasant for hiking during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended, while winter offers a different, often quieter, experience.

Q: Are there any hikes that pass through local villages?
A: Yes, some routes are designed to connect or pass through local settlements. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31479744" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corral del Tío Polo – Embid Castle loop from Cihuela&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, starts from Cihuela and allows you to experience the local village atmosphere.
tips: Walking near the town of Deza, surrounded by cultivated fields and surely bound for the Fuente de Almazorre, we will find an indicative sign.
Walking along the long-distance path, on the way from Serón de Nágima to Deza, we find the Almazorre Fountain. An ideal destination if we are in Deza.

faqs.question: What is the estimated duration for completing the Fuente de Almazorre round trip from Deza?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for completing this hike is approximately 2 hours and 30 minutes. This is based on an average hiking pace for the 9.2 km distance.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this hike?
faqs.answer: This hike is rated as moderate. While some sections in the general area, like parts of the GR-86, are considered easy, this specific round trip from Deza involves a total elevation gain of about 149 meters, contributing to its moderate classification.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the "Fuente de Almazorre by the Camino de Serón de Nágima to Deza – round trip from Deza" is designed as a circular route, meaning you will finish at your starting point in Deza.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead in Deza?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for this exact trailhead are not detailed, Deza is a small municipality. Hikers typically find parking within the village itself, close to the starting point of the route. Look for available street parking or designated public parking areas.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: You can expect a diverse landscape featuring well-preserved Mediterranean arboreal vegetation, including holm oaks ("encinas") and Portuguese oaks ("quejigos"). The trail offers narrow paths, varied open and wooded scenery, and numerous panoramic viewpoints, especially as it accesses different high lines. The environment is part of the LIC Quejigares de Gómara-Nágima natural area.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights on this route include the Fuente de Almazorre and sections of the Trail from Serón de Nágima to Deza. The trail also offers extensive views of the surrounding natural landscape.

faqs.question: Is the Fuente de Almazorre trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails in this region are dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near livestock, and to ensure you clean up after your pet. There are no specific restrictions mentioned for this trail.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this trail is typically during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the vegetation is vibrant. Summer can be very hot in this region, while winter might bring colder temperatures, though the trail is generally accessible year-round.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Fuente de Almazorre trail or other public trails in this region of Soria.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route likely utilizes sections of the broader trail network in the area. Specifically, it covers portions of the Senda de los Arrieros (35% of the route) and a small part of the [CIMA SO07] Alto Cruz (3%). The general area is also known for the GR-86, the Iberian Soriano Trail.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or pubs in Deza after the hike?
faqs.answer: Deza is a small village, but you can typically find local establishments such as bars or small restaurants where you can get refreshments or a meal after your hike. It's advisable to check their opening hours, especially outside of peak tourist seasons.
